tax: Placed at order rank, Glacial Elementals is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: an ice or cold body grown from frozen water, not liquid or vapour. A new family belongs here only if it shows a distinct ice mode with its own cold source and field signs.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a creeping rime-front or a hail-forming cloud where cold collects. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — breath-frost, a rime-sheet, or melting hailstones — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Glacial Elementals occupies cold seeps, ice-rimed galleries, and cold updraft shafts. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where water-pressure freezes a cold seep or chills an updraft and recur with the cold seep refreezes or the updraft re-chills, sustaining themselves on cold-seep water or chilled updraft air.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: members pose as frost or mist and rouse to warmth or intrusion, locking prey or flinging ice. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: an ice body, immunity to cold, and a warmth-failure. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each family and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: freezing-advance, immobilising, and hail-shot. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Glacial Elementals matters because they make the chill tiers an immobilising or ranged-ice hazard. It lets survey reports sort cold hazards by mode, so a crew knows whether to fear ground-frost lock or flung hail without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Glacial Elementals is the order-rank grouping of water elementals whose bodies are ice and cold, ground-bound or airborne within the True Elementals. Its members share a body of frozen or freezing water that grows by chilling its surroundings and fails in warmth, and divide below into family groups by whether the ice creeps along the ground or forms and flings in the air. Glacial elementals are the cold-country water kin, hazards of the chill high tiers.
